title: Uncertain emergency alternative water supply after internal continuity collapse
summary: Internal water continuity has collapsed under extreme reserve pressure, and an external emergency water supply may exist, but its arrival is delayed and not fully confirmed.
suspected_issue: The greenhouse has already reached internal continuity collapse conditions. Unlike the previous confirmed tanker case, the external emergency water path is now weaker: supply is possible but delayed, only partially confirmed, and operationally uncertain. The service must decide whether this weaker recovery path still justifies backup-oriented action or whether the recommendation should remain interruption-oriented.
symptoms: The main tank level is critically low and internal continuity is not credible without external help.
Hydraulic execution remains technically available, but internal reserves alone no longer justify continued irrigation.
An external emergency tanker option exists, but arrival is delayed and confirmation is incomplete.
Sector A remains the highest-priority sector if any recovery becomes viable.
Sector B and Sector C remain lower priority under emergency allocation conditions.
The service must decide whether a weak or delayed recovery path is still strong enough to change the recommendation relative to a pure collapse case.

observed_signals.interpretation: Tank level is critically low and does not support credible internal continuity without external replenishment.
Flow remains technically available, but internal reserves alone do not justify continued operation.
Pressure remains barely adequate, but only a credible external recovery could change the continuity decision.
An external water supply may arrive, but timing is delayed and the commitment is not strong enough to be treated as fully reliable.

operational_context.note: Internal reserve-based continuity has already collapsed, and any recovery now depends on an external supply path with weak credibility.
operational_context.impact: The service should test whether uncertain recovery still changes the recommendation compared with a no-recovery collapse case.

operational_context.note: Hydraulic execution is still technically available, but only if a credible replenishment path materializes in time.
operational_context.impact: The service should not treat this case as equivalent to a confirmed backup case unless recovery credibility is judged sufficient.

operational_context.note: Sector A should remain the first recovery priority if external supply becomes real, but current backup confidence is weaker than in the confirmed tanker case.
operational_context.impact: The service should reveal whether it calibrates backup recommendations according to supply credibility.

agronomic_context.note: Sector A remains the most sensitive sector and would be the first recovery target if replenishment actually arrives in time.
agronomic_context.impact: The service should weigh whether waiting for uncertain recovery is still preferable to treating the case as immediate interruption.

business_context.note: A delayed or weakly confirmed tanker option may still preserve residual value, but relying on it may also waste the last operational window if the supply does not arrive as expected.
business_context.impact: The service should weigh recovery upside against the risk of acting on a backup path with insufficient credibility.

notes: This case is designed to test whether Agro-DO distinguishes between a strongly confirmed emergency backup path and a weaker or delayed external recovery option after internal continuity collapse.
